text:
the UI should be tik-tok like where I only see what set I need to do next and perhaps cues or something that can be attached by the coach for the exercise. [...] - **Coach cues** attached per exercise; **effortless feedback** (Easy/Moderate/Hard pills), quick Note + Skip, big Log & Next.
- Native polish from your skills: SF Symbols, semantic type, safe-area-inset controls, 44pt targets, haptics. [...] - **StepPageView.swift** — single-set page: overline, mini-map (`MiniMapRow`, horizontal-scrollable, current movement marked with `play.fill`), exercise name/set count, hero target text, and the coach cue as a quiet glass pill (`quote.bubble` icon). Hero numbers use `@ScaledMetric` so the large custom sizes still respond to Dynamic Type. Rest pages get their own big countdown treatment.
- **ControlsView.swift** — bottom glass cluster: `GlassEffectContainer`-wrapped Easy/Moderate/Hard `EffortPill`s (each paired with an SF Symbols gauge glyph, 44pt min height), an optional subtle reps `+`/`-` nudge (44×44 tap targets), and a second `GlassEffectContainer` with Note (`square.and.pencil`), Skip (`forward.end.fill`), and the primary `.glassProminent` Log & Next / Finish button with a `.symbolEffect(.bounce)` checkmark. Haptics fire exactly as specified: `.selection()` on effort pick, `.impact(.medium)` on Log & Next. Accessibility labels/values are set on all interactive controls and the mini-map. [...] - **Coach cues** attached per exercise, in a quiet glass quote pill.
- **Effortless feedback**: Easy / Moderate / Hard pills (with haptics), quick reps stepper, Note, Skip.
